framework: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0 (NIST AI 100-1)

completeness_checks.note: All 70 NIST AI RMF subcategory outcome elements evaluated. Each element scored using competency_implication text against WIDAI KSA pool. Both outcome_text and competency_implication preserved in every rationale file.

rationale_documentation.note: Every rationale file validated as well-formed JSON with complete method coverage. All 70 files contain STS primary + 4 secondary methods (BERTScore, NLI, Bi-Encoder, Jaccard) with content-specific significance text. Each file preserves dual provenance (outcome_text + competency_implication).

relationship_type_consistency.subset_of_note: No 'Subset of' relationships. Expected: NIST AI RMF competency implications describe organizational risk management outcomes — they are equal to or broader than individual WIDAI KSAs, never narrower.
relationship_type_consistency.no_relationship_note: Zero no-relationship results. Expected for a risk management framework whose workforce requirements are within WIDAI's core data/AI governance domain.
relationship_type_consistency.strength_mapping_consistency_check: PASS — Equal (mean 7.05) > Superset (mean 6.01) > Intersects (mean 4.1). Monotonically decreasing strength across relationship types, consistent with all five prior STRMs.

strength_scoring.scoring_methodology: Cross-Encoder STS (stsb-roberta-base) as primary for all elements, raw 0-1 mapped to 0-10 integer. Functional rationale per NIST IR 8477.
strength_scoring.function_consistency: PASS — GOVERN (mean 6.87) highest, consistent with WIDAI's strong governance domain coverage. MEASURE lowest (mean 6.43), reflecting measurement-specific vocabulary not fully captured by WIDAI KSAs. All functions within 0.5 of overall mean.

mapping_strength_rationale_consistency.note: All strength values in strm_mapping.json match corresponding rationale files.

comparison_to_prior_strms.note: STRM-006 scored mean (6.62) is the highest across all STRMs. This is expected: (1) competency_implication text was authored to describe workforce competencies implied by risk management outcomes — naturally closer to competency language than raw framework descriptions; (2) NIST AI RMF scope (70 outcomes across 4 functions) is entirely within WIDAI's data/AI governance/risk domain; (3) NIST AI RMF vocabulary strongly overlaps with WIDAI's governance and risk management domains. STRM-006 mean is comparable to STRM-005 (6.34), the other dual-provenance regulatory STRM.
comparison_to_prior_strms.consistency_assessment: CONSISTENT — Higher mean is methodologically explained by dual-provenance approach and narrow domain scope, not scoring drift.

dual_provenance.description: Each rationale file preserves both outcome_text (NIST AI RMF original) and competency_implication (workforce interpretation). Scoring uses competency_implication.

rationale_type_validation.justification: Mapping direction is outcome→competency (what workforce capability achieves this outcome?). This is a Functional question per NIST IR 8477. Consistent with STRM-005 EU AI Act methodology.
rationale_type_validation.status: PASS — Rationale type consistent with STRM-005 and justified by element type

per_fde_detection.criteria: NLI domain mismatch, STS threshold fragility, bi-encoder vocabulary inflation

gap_analysis.note: 14 elements (20.0%) flagged with gap signals. 7 critical domain mismatches — AG gravity well pattern continues (3 of 7 critical gaps matched to AG KSAs). All 70 elements map to at least one WIDAI KSA with strength ≥ 4. Gap signals represent potential domain mismatches, not missing coverage.
gap_analysis.status: PASS — Gap detection ran at pipeline time. 14 gaps documented in gap register.

cross_strm_consistency.rationale_type_deviation: Functional (consistent with STRM-005, deviation from STRM-001–004 Semantic). Justified by outcome→competency mapping direction.
cross_strm_consistency.scoring_method_note: STS-primary for all elements, same as STRM-001–005.
cross_strm_consistency.distribution_note: STRM-006 has 0% No relationship, consistent with STRM-004 (0%) and STRM-005 (0%). Zero no-relationship is expected for narrow, in-domain frameworks.
